untranslatable_reason: This signal is an options spread adjustment strategy that requires multi-leg options position tracking, strike price monitoring, and premium/debit calculations. The backtest engine indicators only support price and technical indicators on a single underlying asset. Required data: tracking open positions (July 50/60/70 calls), buyback execution at specific strikes, net debit accounting, and break-even calculation at expiration. These require an options-specific execution engine, not price-based technical rules.
translation_notes: Signal describes a calendar/volatility adjustment strategy (McMillan Dry strategy). Entry requires closing a bear spread position and establishing a bull spread with specific strike relationships. Exit conditions marked AUTHOR_SILENT require inference. The underlying stock price decline from 60 to 45 is documented but position entry/exit logic depends on options Greeks, implied volatility, and time decay—none of which are available in the allowed indicator set. This is fundamentally an options portfolio management rule, not a price-action trading rule.
